title: Declarative terminal-demo scenario
description: A single self-verifying spec that drives three modes over one demo: `demo-kit lint` (run every step in isolation and assert its output), `demo-kit play` (drive it live, waiting on each assertion), and `demo-kit record` (regenerate a VHS tape and render a GIF/MP4). Authoring a demo as data means the live walk-through and the recorded video can never drift, and every step is proven before it is shown.

title.description: Human-readable title shown on the recording's title card.

output.description: Output basename for record mode (no extension, no slash). Drives the generated tape + render targets: demo/<output>.tape, demo/<output>.gif, demo/<output>.mp4. Defaults to the scenario filename stem (e.g. scenario.killer.json → "scenario.killer", unless overridden to "killer"). Set this to keep a second cut from clobbering the main demo's demo.* outputs.

terminal.description: Presentation knobs for record mode (mapped onto VHS `Set` directives) and shared by play mode where applicable.

theme.description: VHS color theme name, e.g. "Catppuccin Mocha".

typingSpeed.description: Per-character typing delay, e.g. "45ms". Used by VHS `Set TypingSpeed` and as the char-by-char delay in play mode.

workdir.description: Repo-relative working directory for every command. Defaults to ".". Note: the `a11y` shell function uses `pnpm exec`, which requires a package.json on the path, so the repo root is usually correct.

shellInit.description: Lines sourced before every command (e.g. "source demo/_a11y.sh"). Hidden from the screen in record/play; sourced in a Hidden block in the generated tape.

isolation.description: Optional fixture isolation. The runner exposes the fixture path as an env var named by `as`. In `lint`, that var points at a per-run TEMP COPY of `fixture` so mutating steps cannot race other processes or dirty the repo. In `play`/`record`, it points at the real `fixture` path so on-screen commands read clean.

fixture.description: Repo-relative path to the fixture directory to isolate (e.g. "demo/sample-app").

as.description: Env var name the runner binds to the fixture path (e.g. "FIX"). Commands reference it as "$FIX".

setup.description: Hidden housekeeping steps run once before the acts.

teardown.description: Hidden housekeeping steps run once after the acts (and on play-mode exit).

acts.description: The body of the demo, grouped into titled acts.

title.description: Act title (shown as an on-screen comment / section header).

step.description: One demo beat: optional dim narration, a typed-and-executed command, and optional output assertions.

say.description: Dim narration lines printed before the command is typed.

run.description: The shell command typed on screen and executed. May reference the isolation env var (e.g. "$FIX").

expect.description: Assertions evaluated against the captured stdout+stderr and exit code. Any failing assertion makes `lint` fail the step and exit non-zero; `play` waits until they are all satisfied (or times out).

stdoutContains.description: Substrings that MUST all appear in the combined output.

stdoutNotContains.description: Substrings that MUST NOT appear in the combined output (e.g. "ENOENT").

stdoutRegex.description: A regular expression (JS syntax) the combined output MUST match.

read.description: Seconds to pause after output, for pacing. In record mode this is added to a scan allowance to derive the VHS `Sleep`.

hidden.description: When true, the step runs but is not shown on screen (housekeeping inside an act).
